subject: Science
description: generate and interpret graphs and charts describing different types of motion, including the use of real-time technology such as motion detectors or photogates

subject: Science
description: describe and calculate the motion of an object using position, displacement, speed, velocity, and acceleration

subject: Science
description: analyze and describe accelerated motion in two dimensions using equations, including projectile and circular examples

subject: Science
description: research and describe the historical development of the concepts of gravitational, electromagnetic, weak nuclear, and strong nuclear forces

subject: Science
description: describe and calculate how the magnitude of the gravitational force between two objects depends on their masses and the distance between their centers

subject: Science
description: describe and calculate how the magnitude of the electrical force between two objects depends on their charges and the distance between them

subject: Science
description: identify examples of electric and magnetic forces in everyday life

subject: Science
description: describe Newton's three laws of motion and apply them to solve problems involving net force, mass, and acceleration

subject: Science
description: calculate the effect of forces on objects, including the law of inertia, the relationship between force and acceleration, and the nature of force pairs between objects

subject: Science
description: identify and describe motion relative to different frames of reference

subject: Science
description: investigate and calculate quantities using the work-energy theorem in various situations

subject: Science
description: investigate examples of kinetic and potential energy and their transformations

subject: Science
description: calculate the mechanical energy of, power generated within, impulse applied to, and momentum of a physical system

subject: Science
description: demonstrate and apply the laws of conservation of energy and conservation of momentum

subject: Science
description: examine and describe oscillatory motion and wave propagation in various types of media

subject: Science
description: investigate and analyze characteristics of waves, including velocity, frequency, amplitude, and wavelength, and calculate using the relationship between wave speed, frequency, and wavelength

subject: Science
description: compare characteristics and behaviors of transverse waves, including electromagnetic waves, and characteristics and behaviors of longitudinal waves, including sound waves

subject: Science
description: investigate behaviors of waves, including reflection, refraction, diffraction, interference, resonance, and the Doppler effect

subject: Science
description: describe and calculate the relationship between electric current, potential difference, and resistance using Ohm's law

subject: Science
description: analyze series and parallel circuits in terms of current, voltage, and resistance using Ohm's law and Kirchhoff's rules

subject: Science
description: investigate and describe the relationship between electric and magnetic fields in applications such as generators, motors, and transformers
